title: Minecraft Modder Pulse
subtitle: A survey for Minecraft mod developers about the state of their mod, the modding experience, and where the project goes next.

audience_profiles.label: Hobbyist Modder
audience_profiles.description: Building the mod part-time as a passion project.

audience_profiles.label: Solo Modder
audience_profiles.description: Owning most decisions and implementation alone.

audience_profiles.label: Team Maintainer
audience_profiles.description: Working with collaborators on content, engineering, or release flow.

audience_profiles.label: Full-Time Creator
audience_profiles.description: Treating the mod as a major ongoing product or income-supported project.

sections.title: Project Snapshot
sections.description: Ground the response in what the mod is, who builds it, and where it sits right now.

questions.prompt: What is the name of your mod?
questions.description: Use the public name or an internal codename.

questions.prompt: Which loader or platform best describes the main release target?
Which Minecraft versions are you actively supporting or targeting?
Which categories best describe your mod?
What stage is the mod currently in?
How is the mod currently staffed?

sections.title: Current Mod Health
sections.description: Measure confidence, satisfaction, and where the project currently feels strong or fragile.

questions.prompt: How good do you feel about the mod in its current state?
questions.description: Choose the score that best matches your honest feeling right now.

questions.prompt: How likely are you to recommend this mod to players who enjoy its niche?
How confident are you in your next release or update?
Rate the current health of each area of the project.
rows: The core gameplay loop is fun
The current content scope feels coherent
The mod still matches the original vision

questions.prompt: What is creating the most friction for the mod right now?
questions.description: Select every issue that is meaningfully slowing you down.

questions.prompt: Which part of the mod feels strongest right now, and why?
questions.placeholder: Describe the feature, system, or experience you feel best about.

sections.title: Modding Experience
sections.description: Focus on the day-to-day craft of building, debugging, updating, and maintaining the mod.

questions.prompt: How well is the current modding workflow serving you?
questions.description: Rate the parts of the workflow you rely on most.
rows: Working with mappings and API docs

questions.prompt: How often are you currently able to work on or ship meaningful progress?
Where are you on the energy-to-burnout spectrum with this mod?
Rank the improvements that would most improve your modding experience.
questions.description: Drag the most important improvement to the top.

questions.prompt: What part of modding this project still feels harder than it should?
questions.placeholder: Call out one recurring frustration or structural problem.

sections.title: Players and Community
sections.description: Look at player signals, requests, and how clearly the community helps shape the mod.

questions.prompt: Where do you most often receive player feedback?
questions.description: Select all channels that regularly influence development.
options: I do not have a strong feedback loop yet

questions.prompt: How clearly can you tell what players actually want next?
About how many actionable bug reports, suggestions, or issues do you get in a typical week?
Rate how well different player groups are currently served by the mod.
rows: New players can understand the mod quickly
Experienced players have enough depth to explore
Server owners can configure it reliably
Modpack makers can integrate it cleanly

questions.prompt: Rank the kinds of player requests you feel most pressure to act on.
What player reaction, story, or use case made you feel the mod was worth building?
questions.placeholder: Share one memorable player moment or response.

sections.title: Future of the Mod
sections.description: Close on roadmap clarity, sustainability, and what success looks like from here.

questions.prompt: Which direction best describes the next phase of the mod?
Which areas are most likely to define your next season of work?
questions.description: Select all that are likely to matter.

questions.prompt: How confident are you that the mod has a healthy future?
How strongly do you agree with the following statements about the mod's future?
rows: I can realistically keep maintaining this mod for the next 12 months
I know which features should come next
Community demand still justifies the investment
The current codebase can support the roadmap

questions.prompt: What is the single biggest thing that could stop this mod from reaching the future you want for it?
questions.placeholder: Describe the biggest blocker as concretely as you can.

questions.prompt: If this mod goes well over the next year, what would success look like to you?
questions.placeholder: Describe the outcome that would make the effort feel worth it.

guided_presets.label: Solo Burnout Check
guided_presets.description: Jump into workload, motivation, friction, and the future of a mostly one-person project.

guided_presets.label: Release Readiness
guided_presets.description: Focus on stability, shipping confidence, onboarding, and issue pressure before the next update.

guided_presets.label: Community Signal Pass
guided_presets.description: Surface questions about player feedback, request pressure, and who the mod is serving well.

guided_presets.label: Future of the Mod
guided_presets.description: Go straight to roadmap clarity, sustainability, blockers, and success criteria.

faq_items.question: How does Random Mode calculate the odds for this survey?
faq_items.answer: Random Mode samples uniformly from the eligible question pool. There are {eligible_question_count} eligible questions across {eligible_section_count} section(s) in {whitelist_summary}, so each question currently has {per_question_fraction} = {per_question_probability} chance per spin. {section_probability_breakdown}

faq_items.question: What does Guided Match actually do?
faq_items.answer: Guided Match does not permanently hide content. It uses your creator profile and selected topics to surface the highest-signal questions first, so you can move quickly to the areas most relevant to your mod right now.
